OpenTopography high-resolution topography data and tools

goal: Build complete photorealistic Unreal river environments for South Fork American, Colorado River, and Pacuare River from traceable maps, seasonal flow evidence, aerial imagery, reviewed reference media, and procedural generation where appropriate.

rivers.display_name: South Fork American River, Chili Bar to Coloma

source_sample_artifacts.provider: USDA/APFO NAIP ImageServer
source_sample_artifacts.unreal_use: provenance smoke-test sample retained; active preview drape now uses usda_naip_chili_bar_corridor_sample_1024

source_sample_artifacts.provider: RaftSim review artifact from multi-year CDEC CBR event-flow context
source_sample_artifacts.unreal_use: Representative seasonal flow evidence for future low/median/high and unsafe-flow visual variants; not final water width, foam, wet-bank, hole stickiness, feature forcing, gameplay tuning, or lifelike-water authority.

source_sample_artifacts.provider: RaftSim review artifact from CDEC CBR/A25 context and South Fork flow presets
source_sample_artifacts.unreal_use: Flow-band discussion aid for future low/median/high water variants; not final water width, foam, wet-bank, hole, or gameplay tuning authority.

source_sample_artifacts.provider: RaftSim review artifact from State Parks and county GIS source leads
source_sample_artifacts.unreal_use: Controls access-context and publication-sensitivity review before detailed South Fork maps, screenshots, rescue routes, or access gameplay are promoted; no third-party media or sensitive geometry is imported.

source_sample_artifacts.provider: RaftSim generated link-only media review annotation scaffold
source_sample_artifacts.unreal_use: Editor-visible review seed points for visual fidelity, flow context, rescue readability, and guide/outfitter discussion; points come from stationing scaffolds and cannot drive production assets until editor placement, item rights, and guide review pass.

source_sample_artifacts.provider: RaftSim generated link-only media rights gate
source_sample_artifacts.unreal_use: Blocks texture, photogrammetry, AI-training, packaged media, marketing, or production-art use until item-level rights and guide review are recorded.

source_sample_artifacts.provider: RaftSim generated review scaffolds from South Fork access review and NHD stationing candidate
source_sample_artifacts.unreal_use: Editor overlays for access, no-publish, and evacuation/rescue review; not final access geometry or screenshot clearance.

source_sample_artifacts.promotion_blocker: Consolidates Pacuare source metadata only; lifelike production remains blocked on high-resolution imagery, official geometry, numeric flow evidence, access/protected-area clearance, media rights, guide review, and in-engine capture evidence.
Scene metadata identifies official Landsat/Sentinel candidates only; no imagery product is downloaded or approved for photoreal Pacuare materials, masks, or lifelike captures.
No Landsat browse image, metadata JSON, COG band, or QA mask is attached; anonymous asset URLs return EROS login HTML and require an approved access workflow before Unreal source-drape replacement.
SINAC Cobertura Forestal 2023 is useful for future canopy masks and procedural foliage zones only after terms review; no direct SNIT orthophoto source-drape replacement is attached.

Thumbnails and tile metadata are attached for review only; full-resolution COG bands, SCL masks, corridor clipping, manual QA, and guide/geospatial approval are still required before Unreal source-drape or material replacement.

Sentinel COG assets are reachable with byte-range reads, but no full bands or derivatives are committed; Pacuare source-drape replacement still needs raster-window extraction, corridor clipping, SCL/cloud QA, terms review, and guide/geospatial approval.

This is a downsampled full-tile 17PKM TCI review image, not a Pacuare corridor clip, source drape, mask source, or lifelike evidence; production still needs stitched 17PKM/16PHR corridor COG reads, SCL/cloud QA, terms review, and guide/geospatial approval.

This is a downsampled full-tile 16PHR TCI review image, not a Pacuare corridor clip, source drape, mask source, or lifelike evidence; production still needs stitched 17PKM/16PHR corridor COG reads, SCL/cloud QA, terms review, and guide/geospatial approval.

This is a bbox-level tile coverage review, not a corridor clip, source drape, mask source, material input, or lifelike evidence; production still needs exact native-CRS clipping, stitched COG window reads, SCL/cloud QA, terms review, and guide/geospatial approval.

These are draft planning-bounds bbox window clips, not exact route clips, source drapes, mask sources, material inputs, or lifelike evidence; production still needs exact native-CRS route/bank clipping, stitched COG reads, SCL/cloud QA, terms review, and guide/geospatial approval.
SCL QA previews show the current January 19, 2025 Pacuare bbox clips are cloud/shadow affected; they are QA references only, not production drapes, masks, material inputs, or lifelike evidence.

This search identifies the next cleaner Sentinel candidate pair only; production still needs SCL-first QA, exact clipping, stitching, terms review, and guide/geospatial approval before Unreal source-drape replacement.
March 20, 2025 SCL QA passes for matching TCI review-clip download only; production still needs TCI clip review, exact route/bank clipping, stitching, terms review, and guide/geospatial approval.
March 20, 2025 TCI clips are draft bbox review windows only; production still needs exact route/bank clipping, SCL masking, stitching, terms review, and guide/geospatial approval.

Draft-bank strip review shows route-local cloud/shadow and uses preview-scaffold bank geometry; production still needs cleaner route-local coverage or exact approved route/bank clipping with SCL masking, stitching, terms review, and guide/geospatial approval.
March 5, 2025 route-local SCL is worse than March 20, so no TCI clips should be downloaded for this scene.
February 4 route-local SCL was marginally competitive, but companion TCI review confirms visible cloud/shadow contamination; do not use for route-window masking or source-drape promotion.

February 4 TCI route-window strips remain visibly cloud/shadow contaminated and are review-only; continue cleaner-scene search or exact route-window SCL-masked stitching before any source-drape decision.
February 24 route-local SCL is much worse than February 4 and March 20, so no TCI clips should be downloaded for this scene.
